page: Amazon Web Services m8ib.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by Intel processors. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance provides 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with a dedicated allocation model. The processors operate at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, delivering high-frequency execution capabilities for complex computational tasks.

The instance is configured with 768.0 GB of system memory, which translates to a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include any local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met using network-attached block storage. This hardware profile offers a high resource density suitable for workloads that require significant memory capacity alongside massive parallel processing power.

With a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, the m8ib.48xlarge supports high-throughput data transfers and low-latency communication. This network capacity, combined with the large compute and memory footprint, makes the instance well-suited for enterprise-grade databases, large-scale containerized microservices, high-traffic web serving, and distributed caching layers.
description: Amazon Web Services m8ib.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by Intel processors on the x86_64 architecture. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, requiring external storage attachments for persistent data. With a high baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, this instance is designed for demanding enterprise workloads, large-scale web serving, container orchestration, and database deployments that require substantial compute and memory resources without local storage dependencies.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8ib.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 Intel vCPUs, 768 GB of RAM, and a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, running on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage.

tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance offering massive Intel compute power, balanced memory, and high-speed networking for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 Intel vCPUs and 96 physical cores running at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
